trivia_bullets.text: Part of Core Set 2021 (M21), released July 3, 2020. Blue-black graveyard was one of M21's ten explicit two-color limited archetypes; Archfiend's Vessel and Obsessive Stitcher were named as its key payoff cards.
sources: Hipsters of the Coast: Core Set 2021 Limited Preview (2020)

trivia_bullets.text: The most consistent reanimation enablers identified by tournament players were Lurrus of the Dream-Den (companion) and Call of the Death-Dweller (which could put two copies back on turn 3 for two 5/5 demons). Some evaluations noted the setup cost was significant and the payoff 'mediocre' relative to other reanimation targets — community sentiment, labeled as such.
sources: Star City Games: Quest For The Archfiend (2020)

trivia_bullets.text: Illustrated by Jake Murray, a Columbus, Ohio-based fantasy illustrator who sells prints of the Archfiend's Vessel art through his INPRNT storefront. The card has no flavor text in any printing.
sources: INPRNT: Jake Murray Studio Ltd gallery

trivia_bullets.text: Legal in Pioneer, Modern, Legacy, Vintage, and Commander. EDHREC rank 13,910 reflects modest Commander inclusion — the five-mana-token payoff competes against established graveyard pieces.

related_cards.name: Obsessive Stitcher
related_cards.note: Designed as the blue-black graveyard lane partner in M21 limited; loots to fill graveyard feeding Vessel

related_cards.name: Call of the Death-Dweller
related_cards.note: The reanimation spell most commonly paired to trigger Vessel's exile-and-replace ability

related_cards.name: Lurrus of the Dream-Den
related_cards.note: Companion shell that enabled recurring cheap casts of Vessel from the graveyard in Pioneer lists
